description: Clusters derived from German Federal Statistical Office classification with modifications for applied social sciences

clusters.label: Religious Studies & Theology
notes: Merges Protestant Theology, Catholic Theology, Islamic Studies, Jewish Studies, Religious Studies, Comparative Religion.
Includes confessional theologies, biblical studies, church history, religious ethics.
EXCLUSION: Empirical psychology of religion → Psychology; sociology of religion → Sociology.

clusters.label: Philosophy
notes: Includes history of philosophy, logic, ethics, epistemology, metaphysics, political philosophy.
Experimental Philosophy is a field within this cluster.
EXCLUSION: Purely normative bioethics in medical context → Human Medicine.

clusters.label: History
notes: Includes ancient, medieval, modern, contemporary, regional, economic, and social history.
History of science/technology included here unless clearly about present-day science policy.
EXCLUSION: Archaeological methods → Archaeology (within History).

clusters.label: General & Comparative Literature and Linguistics
notes: General/comparative literature, general/comparative linguistics, applied linguistics.
Computer linguistics, foreign language training, Indo-European studies.
EXCLUSION: Language-specific studies → respective philology cluster.

clusters.label: Classical Philology
notes: Greek Philology, Latin Philology, Byzantine Studies, Papyrology, Old Greek, Classical Linguistics.
Focus on ancient Greek and Roman languages, literature, and culture.

clusters.label: German Studies
notes: German linguistics and literature; German as a foreign/second language.
Dutch Studies, Scandinavian Studies, other Germanic languages (excl. English).

clusters.label: English & American Studies
notes: English linguistics and literature; American Studies; didactics of English.
Business English and specialised English included.

clusters.label: Romance Studies
notes: French, Italian, Spanish, Ibero-Romance, Portuguese, other Romance languages and literatures.

clusters.label: Slavonic, Baltic & Finno-Ugrian Studies
notes: Slavonic (East/West/South), Baltic, Finno-Ugrian linguistics and literatures.
Includes Russian, Polish, Czech, Balkan Studies, Sorabistics.

clusters.label: Other Linguistics & Cultural Studies
notes: African, Asian, Middle Eastern, Turkic, and other non-European linguistics and cultural studies.
Includes Egyptology, Sinology, Japanese Studies, Korean Studies, Indian Studies, etc.
EXCLUSION: Religious-studies-focused work → Religious Studies & Theology.

clusters.label: Cultural Studies
notes: Cultural studies in the narrower sense; ethnology, European ethnology, folklore studies.
EXCLUSION: Media/cultural industries with social-science focus → Media & Communication.

clusters.label: Regional Studies
notes: Area studies: African, Latin American, Near/Middle Eastern, North American, European, East Asian, South Asian, Southeast Asian Studies.
Interdisciplinary study of specific world regions.

clusters.label: Media & Communication Studies
notes: Merged Media Studies and Communication Studies/Journalism.
Includes journalism studies, mass communication, media content analysis, media effects.
EXCLUSION: Pure literary/film analysis → relevant literature/arts cluster.
EXCLUSION: Political communication with clear political-science framing → Political Science.

clusters.label: Political Science
notes: Political systems, political theory, comparative politics, international relations, political behaviour.
Political communication can be coded here if primarily about political processes/institutions.
EXCLUSION: Political psychology with psychological theory → Psychology.

clusters.label: Sociology & Social Sciences
notes: General sociology, social theory, demography, methods in sociology, special sociologies.
Social structure, institutions, networks, stratification, inequality.
EXCLUSION: Individual-level psychological constructs → Psychology.
EXCLUSION: Large-scale attitude surveys without sociological theory → may be Psychology or Political Science.

clusters.label: Social Work & Social Services
notes: Social work, social pedagogy, social services, social welfare practice.
Applied intervention research in social service contexts.

clusters.label: Psychology
notes: All branches of scientific psychology: general/cognitive, social, clinical, developmental, biological/neuro, educational, occupational/organisational, personality, methods.
Field-level breakdown handled separately for clusters with >50 papers.
EXCLUSION: Psychiatry and psychotherapy in medical settings → Human Medicine.

clusters.label: Education
notes: Educational science, schooling, teacher training, curriculum, empirical education research.
Education policy, adult education, early childhood education, special education.
EXCLUSION: Discipline-specific teaching (e.g., teaching statistics in psychology) → the discipline.
EXCLUSION: Educational psychology → Psychology.

clusters.label: Law
notes: Civil, public, criminal, labour, tax, EU and international law, comparative law.
Philosophy of law, history of law, sociology of law.
EXCLUSION: Clearly sociological law-and-society work → Sociology.

clusters.label: Public Administration
notes: Public management, administrative science, civil service studies.
Government operations and policy implementation.

clusters.label: Economics & Business
notes: Economics, econometrics, business administration, public finance, international economics.
Includes Marketing as a field (consumer behaviour goes here unless explicitly psychological theory).
Includes Behavioural/Experimental Economics when framed with economic constructs (utility, incentives, markets, game theory).
EXCLUSION: General JDM (heuristics, biases) without economic framing → Psychology (Cognitive).

clusters.label: Sport Sciences
notes: Sport science, sport pedagogy, sport medicine (non-clinical), sport psychology, sport sociology.

clusters.label: Health Sciences (General)
notes: Public health, health promotion, health management, health economics (if not clearly economics).
Nursing science and non-medical health professions.

clusters.label: Mathematics
notes: Pure and applied mathematics: algebra, analysis, geometry, topology, stochastics.
EXCLUSION: Applied stats as a tool in a specific field → that field.

clusters.label: Statistics
notes: Statistics as a mathematical/methodological discipline.
EXCLUSION: Applied statistics in a substantive discipline → that discipline.

clusters.label: Physics & Astronomy
notes: Theoretical, experimental, applied physics; astrophysics; astronomy.

clusters.label: Chemistry
notes: Inorganic, organic, physical, analytical chemistry, macromolecular chemistry.

clusters.label: Pharmacy
notes: Pharmaceutical chemistry, technology, biology, pharmacology, clinical pharmacy.

clusters.label: Biology
notes: Botany, zoology, microbiology, genetics, ecology, neurobiology, biotechnology.

clusters.label: Earth Sciences
notes: Geology, geophysics, geochemistry, meteorology, oceanography, hydrology.

clusters.label: Geography
notes: Physical and human geography, regional geography, landscape ecology, geoinformatics.

clusters.label: Human Medicine
notes: Pre-clinical, clinical-theoretical, and clinical-practical human medicine.
Includes psychiatry, medical psychology, biomedical research.

clusters.label: Dentistry
notes: Pre-clinical and clinical dentistry, orthodontics, dental surgery.

clusters.label: Veterinary Medicine
notes: Pre-clinical, clinical-theoretical, and clinical-practical veterinary medicine.

clusters.label: Landscape Management & Environmental Architecture
notes: Landscape architecture, landscape ecology, landscape planning, nature conservation.

clusters.label: Agriculture
notes: Plant production, animal husbandry, agricultural engineering, agricultural economics, horticulture.

clusters.label: Forestry & Wood Management
notes: Forestry, forest science, wood management, wood science.

clusters.label: Nutritional & Domestic Science
notes: Nutritional science, household science, food science.

clusters.label: Industrial Engineering
notes: Industrial engineering with engineering focus (not business informatics).

clusters.label: Mining & Metallurgy
notes: Mining engineering, mineral resources, metallurgy and foundry engineering.

clusters.label: Mechanical & Process Engineering
notes: Mechanical engineering, production, manufacturing, process engineering.

clusters.label: Electrical, Computer & Information Engineering
notes: Electrical engineering, power systems, communications engineering, embedded systems.

clusters.label: Transport Engineering & Nautical Science
notes: Transport systems engineering, automotive, aerospace, shipbuilding, nautical science.

clusters.label: Architecture
notes: Architecture, building design, urban design (design aspect).

clusters.label: Spatial Planning
notes: Urban, regional, and spatial planning, infrastructure planning, environmental planning.

clusters.label: Civil Engineering
notes: Structural engineering, construction engineering, hydraulics, transport infrastructure.

clusters.label: Surveying & Geoinformation
notes: Surveying, cartography, photogrammetry, geodesy.

clusters.label: Computer Science
notes: All branches of computer science: AI, software engineering, theoretical CS, bioinformatics.
Business informatics for computer scientists is here; for economists → Economics & Business.

clusters.label: Materials Science & Materials Engineering
notes: Materials science and engineering of metals, polymers, ceramics, composites.

clusters.label: Engineering (General & Interdisciplinary)
notes: Interdisciplinary engineering topics, mechatronics, renewable energies.

clusters.label: Fine Arts
notes: Painting, sculpture, photography, performance, new media art.

clusters.label: Design
notes: Product, communication, graphic, fashion, textile, jewellery design.

clusters.label: Performing Arts, Film & Theatre
notes: Theatre, acting, directing, dance, film and television production, theatre studies.

clusters.label: Music & Musicology
notes: Music performance, composition, conducting, church music, music education, musicology.

clusters.label: Art & Art Theory
notes: Art history, art education, art theory, restoration, art therapy.

clusters.label: Science Studies / Theory of Science
notes: Interdisciplinary study of science as a social or epistemic system.
Metascience, research on research, science policy, scientometrics.
USE ONLY when the article analyses science in general, not a specific discipline's methods.
EXCLUSION: Discipline-specific methods papers → that discipline.
EXCLUSION: Replication studies in psychology → Psychology; in economics → Economics.
